    98→        Dim strReturn As String = ""
    99→        Try
   100→            Dim hexv As String, na As Long
   101→
   102→            hexv = ""
   103→            Do While DecNum > 0
   104→                na = DecNum Mod 16
   105→                Select Case na
   106→                    Case 10
   107→                        hexv = "A" + Trim(hexv)
   108→                    Case 11
   109→                        hexv = "B" + Trim(hexv)
   110→                    Case 12
   111→                        hexv = "C" + Trim(hexv)
   112→                    Case 13
   113→                        hexv = "D" + Trim(hexv)
   114→                    Case 14
   115→                        hexv = "E" + Trim(hexv)
   116→                    Case 15
   117→                        hexv = "F" + Trim(hexv)
   118→                    Case Else
   119→                        hexv = Str(na) + Trim(hexv)
   120→                End Select
   121→                If DecNum >= 16 Then
   122→                    DecNum = Int(DecNum / 16)
   123→                Else
   124→                    hexv = "0" + Trim(hexv)
   125→                    Exit Do
   126→                End If
   127→            Loop
   128→
   129→            If Len(hexv) > 1 And (Len(hexv) Mod 2 = 1) Then
   130→                strReturn = Mid(hexv, 2)
   131→            Else
   132→                strReturn = hexv
   133→            End If
   134→        Catch ex As Exception
   135→            'RemoteLog(MethodBase.GetCurrentMethod().Name & ", ErrorNumber: " & Err.Number & ", Desc: " & Err.Description)
   136→        End Try
   137→        Dec2Hex = strReturn

   370→        Private isPerfCounterSupported As Boolean = False
   371→        Private timerFrequency As Int64 = 0
   372→        Declare Function QueryPerformanceCounter Lib "Kernel32" (ByRef X As Long) As Short

   374→
   375→        Public Sub New()
   376→            Dim returnVal As Integer = QueryPerformanceFrequency(timerFrequency)
   377→            If returnVal <> 0 AndAlso timerFrequency <> 1000 Then
   378→                isPerfCounterSupported = True
   379→            Else
   380→                timerFrequency = 1000
   381→            End If
   382→        End Sub

   384→        Public ReadOnly Property Frequency() As Int64
   385→            Get

   390→        Public ReadOnly Property Value() As Int64
   391→            Get
   392→                Dim tickCount As Int64 = 0
   393→
   394→                If isPerfCounterSupported Then
   395→                    QueryPerformanceCounter(tickCount)
   396→                    Return tickCount
   397→                Else
   398→                    Return CType(Environment.TickCount, Int64)
   399→                End If
   400→            End Get
   401→        End Property

   759→    Private Function ASC2STR(ByVal str As String) As String
   760→
   761→        Dim One_Hex, Tmp_Hex, retVal As String
   762→        Dim i As Integer
   763→
   764→        retVal = ""
   765→        For i = 1 To Len(str)
   766→            One_Hex = Mid(str, i, 1)
   767→            If IsNumeric(One_Hex) Then
   768→                Tmp_Hex = Mid(str, i, 2)
   769→                i = i + 1
   770→            Else
   771→                Tmp_Hex = Mid(str, i, 4)
   772→                i = i + 3
   773→            End If
   774→            retVal = retVal & Chr("&H" & Tmp_Hex)
   775→        Next
   776→        Return retVal
   777→
   778→    End Function

# NX4C FRT ASSEMBLE Project Analysis

**Solution:** `NX4C FRT ASSEMBLE.sln`
**Path:** `C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\DSC CHINA NX4\NX4C FRT\NX4C FRT\`
**Platform:** VB.NET (.NET Framework 4.8), WinForms
**Generated:** 2026-03-13

---

## 1. Projects in Solution

| Project | File | Assembly Name | Role |
|---------|------|---------------|------|
| FRT JA01 | `JA01\FRT JA01.vbproj` | NX4C_FRT_JA01(20211014).exe | Station 1 — lower support barcode check + laser length inspection |
| FRT JA02 | `JA02\FRT JA02.vbproj` | NX4C_FRT_JA02(20250723).exe | Station 2 — SAB/CSAB resistance + torque check + RECL motor tightening |
| FRT JA03 | `JA03\FRT JA03.vbproj` | NX4C_FRT_JA03.exe | Station 3 — recliner operation waveform (amp/angle/speed) inspection |
| FRT JA04 | `JA04\FRT JA04.vbproj` | NX4C_FRT_JA04.exe | Station 4 — recliner harness inspection + air-tool tightening count |
| SERVEr Search | `SERVER SEARCH\SERVEr Search.vbproj` | SERVEr Search.exe | Standalone DB viewer — queries SQL Server, exports to Excel |
| FTECH_SETUP | `FTECH_SETUP\FTECH_SETUP.vdproj` | Setup installer | Install package |

---

## 2. File Structure per Project

All four station projects (JA01–JA04) share an identical folder layout:

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `FrmMain.vb` | Main form: state machine, I/O logic, serial comms, measurement judgment |
| `FrmMain.Designer.vb` | Auto-generated form layout |
| `Module1.vb` | Global variables, BASIC/Port/Barcode data load/save to MDB |
| `Common/MDBOperation.vb` | DB connection helpers (Local MDB + SQL Server) |
| `FrmPort.vb` | Serial port settings form |
| `FrmPart.vb` | Part master CRUD form |
| `FrmSearch.vb` | Local MDB record search + FlexCell grid display |
| `FrmWorkStandardSet.vb` | Inspection standard (BASIC) parameter settings |
| `FrmImage.vb` | Work instruction image popup |
| `FrmManual.vb` | Manual I/O operation form (JA01, JA03, JA04 only) |
| `FrmBarcode.vb` | Barcode label layout settings (JA02, JA03, JA04) |
| `FrmAngleSet.vb` | Recliner angle calibration settings (JA03 only) |
| `AssemblyInfo.vb` | Assembly metadata |

**SERVER SEARCH** files:

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `FrmMain.vb` | SQL Server query form + FlexCell grid binding + Excel export |
| `Module1.vb` | Entry point |

---

## 3. Communication Protocols

### 3-1. Serial Protocol Summary

| Device | Variable | Baud | Data Bits | Port Config | Direction |
|--------|----------|------|-----------|-------------|-----------|
| I/O Module | `Serial_Module` | 115200 | 8 | Configurable via DB | Full-duplex |
| Barcode Scanner | `Serial_Scanner` | 9600 | 8 | Configurable via DB | Receive only |
| Label Printer | `Serial_Printer` | 9600 | 8 | Configurable via DB | Send only |
| Laser Sensor 1 | `Serial_Laser1` | 9600 | 8 | JA01 only (LASER1 port) | Req/Resp |
| Laser Sensor 2 | `Serial_Laser2` | 9600 | 8 | JA01 only (LASER2 port) | Req/Resp |
| Air Tool 1 | `Serial_Tool1` | 9600 | 8 | JA02 only | Receive only |
| Air Tool 2 | `Serial_Tool2` | 9600 | 8 | JA02 only | Receive only |
| Resistance Meter | `Serial_Resist` | 9600 | 8 | JA02 only (RESIST port) | Receive only |

### 3-2. I/O Module Protocol (Serial_Module)

**Frame format — Receive (PC ← Module):**

```
S [32 bits of I/O state] E
```

- Total length: 66 characters
- Start byte: `S` (position 1)
- End byte: `E` (position 66)
- Positions 2–33: DI values (1 char per bit, `0` or `1`)
- Positions 34–65: DO echo values
- Parsed in `ThreadTask2()`: `D_Value(0..31) = CInt(Mid(RecvString, i+2, 1))`

**Frame format — Send (PC → Module):**

```
S [16 bits DO state] [16 bits padding] E
```

- Total: 34 characters
- Initialized as `"S" & "00000000" & "00000000" & "E"`
- DO bits modified via `DO_ON(num)` / `DO_OFF(num)` then written by `DO_WRITE()`

**Polling:** I/O module thread (`ThreadTask2`) reads continuously. DO commands sent on-demand from state machine (100 ms timer).

### 3-3. Laser Sensor Protocol (JA01 only)

**Command (PC → Laser):** `MS<CR><LF>` — issued every ~0.5 s from `Tmr_Work1` timer

**Response format:**
```
MS,01,+19.690,01,+09.672
```
- Length: 25 characters per frame (2-channel response)
- Channel 1 value: positions 7–12 (6 chars, e.g. `+19.690`)
- Channel 2 value: positions 18–23 (6 chars)
- Calibration: `LAser = 100 - CDbl(rawValue)` (distance from 100 mm reference)
- Laser1 → Front-Right, Front-Left channels
- Laser2 → Rear-Right, Rear-Left channels

**Polling interval:** ~0.5 s (LaserCount accumulates in 100 ms timer, triggers at > 0.5)

### 3-4. Label Printer Protocol (Zebra ZPL)

All stations print ZPL labels via `Serial_Printer.Write(...)`.

**JA01 format (fixed layout):**
```
^XA
^FO90,100^BY2,5.0^BXN,5,200,,,,^FD{SerialNumber}^FS
^FO220,60^A0N,60,40^FD{PartNo}^FS
^FO220,120^A0N,60,20^FD{SerialNumber}^FS
^FO220,180^A0N,40,20^FD{PartName}^FS
^XZ
```

**JA02/JA03/JA04 format (configurable layout via Table_Barcode):**
```
^XA
^FO{B1X},{B1Y}^BQ,2,{B1L}^FDQA,{barcode}^FS   (QR code)
^FO{B2X},{B2Y}^A0N,{B2H},{B2W}^FD{barcode}^FS
^FO{B3X},{B3Y}^A0N,{B3H},{B3W}^FDPART NO. : {partno}^FS
^FO{B4X},{B4Y}^A0N,{B4H},{B4H}^FDOPTION : {partname}^FS
^FO{B5X},{B5Y}^A0N,,{B5H},{B5W}^FDDATE : {date}^FS
^FO{B6X},{B6Y}^A0N,,{B6H},{B6W}^FDCAR : {car}^FS
^XZ
```

## 4. State Machine (wStep / rStep / eStep)

All station projects implement a flat floating-point step state machine driven by a 100 ms `Tmr_Work1` timer.

### 4-1. Common Step Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `wStep` | Work sequence step |
| `rStep` | Reset sequence step |
| `eStep` | E-Stop sequence step |
| `wCount` | Delay/count accumulator (seconds, ×0.1 per tick) |
| `rCount` | Reset hold time accumulator |

### 4-2. E-Stop Logic (all stations)

| Step | Condition | Action |
|------|-----------|--------|
| E-Stop trigger | `D_Value(2) = 1` | Show `lblEMOStop`, set `eStep = 1` |
| E-Stop clear | `eStep=1` AND `D_Value(1)=1` | Hide `lblEMOStop`, `eStep = 0` |

### 4-3. Reset Sequence (all stations)

| rStep | Condition to advance | Action |
|-------|---------------------|--------|
| 0 → 1 | `D_Value(1) = 1` held ≥ 2 s | — |
| 1 → 2 | — | DO_ON: 0,2,4,6,8,10; DO_WRITE |
| 2 → 3 | `D_Value(3)=1` AND `D_Value(5)=1` AND `D_Value(1)=0` AND `D_Value(23)=1` | DO_OFF all; DO_WRITE |
| 3 → 0 | — | `Init_Control()`, `wStep = 0` |

### 4-4. JA01 Work Sequence (Laser Length Inspection)

| wStep | Name | Action / Condition to advance |
|-------|------|-------------------------------|
| 0 | Idle | Set message "Scan the Part Barcode." → wStep=1 |
| 1 | Barcode wait | Wait for scanner input |
| 2 | Barcode received | Check LH/RH orientation via D_Value(8) or D_Value(7) |
| 2.1 | Part confirmed | Load BASIC spec values, generate serial number → wStep=3 |
| 2.2 | Position NG | Show alarm, NG sound → wStep=9000 (halt) |
| 3–4 | Pass-through | Direct advance |
| 5 | Wait clamp input | Wait `D_Value(0)=1` → DO_ON(1,11), wStep=6 |
| 6 | Clamp active | Wait `D_Value(4)=1 AND D_Value(0)=0` → DO_OFF(1,11), wStep=6.1 |
| 6.1–6.4 | Clip operation | Upper/Lower clip actuation based on OPTION_UPPER_CLIP/LOWER_CLIP |
| 6.5 | LSUPT check | Check OPTION_LSUPT sensor (NX4/PLMA/NA) → wStep=7 |
| 7 | Start condition check | Check barcode OK + air tool count OK → DO_OFF(2), DO_ON(3), wStep=8 |
| 7.1–7.4 | PLMA sub-steps | PLMA-specific DO sequence |
| 8 | Wait release | `D_Value(6)=1` → DO_OFF(3), wStep=9 |
| 9 | Settle delay | wCount ≥ 1.5 s → wStep=10 |
| 10 | Measurement | Calculate Front/Rear length from laser values + offsets; judge vs spec → wStep=11 |
| 11 | Judgment | Both Front+Rear OK → DingDong, wStep=12; else NG, back to wStep=7 |
| 12–13 | Eject prep | DO_ON(2), wait `D_Value(5)=1` → DO_OFF(2), wStep=14 |
| 14 | Final judgment | All checks OK → Save_DB, Print barcode, wStep=15 |
| 15–16 | Part unload | DO_ON(0,4,6,10), wait sensor, DO_OFF all, wStep=17 |
| 17 | Complete | `Init_Control()`, show FrmImage, wStep=0 |

### 4-5. JA02 Work Sequence (SAB/CSAB + RECL Tightening)

| wStep | Name | Action |
|-------|------|--------|
| 0 | Idle | → wStep=1 |
| 1 | Barcode wait | Accept 23/26/27 char serial number |
| 2 | Part loaded | → wStep=2.01 |
| 2.01 | SAB SN scan wait | Check clamp sensor |
| 2.02 | SAB SN scan | Validate SAB/CSAB barcode vs target |
| 2.1 | SAB check passed | → wStep=3 |
| 3 | Resistance test | Wait resistance meter result → wStep=4 |
| 4 | RECL motor scan | Validate RECL motor barcode → wStep=5 |
| 5 | RECL ready | → wStep=6 |
| 6 | Tightening | Tool1/Tool2 tightening sequence |
| 7 | Complete | Save_DB, print label, → wStep=0 |

### 4-6. JA03 Work Sequence (Recliner Waveform Inspection)

JA03 has a long sub-step sequence (7.1–9.5, 10–12) measuring recliner motor current (amplitude), angle, and speed in both FWD/BWD directions. Key steps:

| wStep Range | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| 0–2 | Idle + barcode scan |
| 6.1 | Harness barcode scan |
| 7.1–7.9 | FWD stroke: current sampling + angle/speed calc |
| 8.0–8.2 | End-angle check |
| 9.1–9.5 | BWD stroke: same measurement |
| 10–11.5 | LSUPT FWD/BWD sub-check |
| 12 | All OK → Save_DB, print label |

### 4-7. JA04 Work Sequence (Harness + Air Tool Count)

Similar structure to JA03 but simplified; measures recliner motor operation and counts air tool fastener completions. Saves to local `TABLE_MAIN`.

## 8. Data Flow

```
[Barcode Scanner (9600)]
        |
        v
Serial_Scanner_DataReceived()
        |
  Validate length (11/13/14 or 23/26/27 chars)
        |
  Load_Part() -- query Table_Part from DB.mdb
        |
  Set OPTION_* flags, generate serial number
        |
        v
[I/O Module (115200)] <-------> ThreadTask2() -- parse 66-char frame
        |                               |
   D_Value[0..31]              State machine (Tmr_Work1, 100ms)
        |                               |
        v                        Check sensors (D_Value)
   DO_ON/DO_OFF()                       |
   DO_WRITE() ----------------------> Serial_Module.Write(D_OutString)
        |
        v
[Laser Sensor (9600)] -- request "MS\r\n" every 0.5s
        |
  ThreadTask1/3() -- parse 25-char response
        |
  LAser1..4 = 100 - rawValue  (offset calibration)
        |
        v
  Length calculation:
    srcLength_Front = BASIC_LENGTH_FRONT_CENTER
                    + (LAser1 + FRONT_RIGHT_OFFSET)
                    + (LAser3 + FRONT_LEFT_OFFSET)
        |
  Judge: (value >= ST + MIN) AND (value <= ST + MAX)
        |
        v
  Decision: OK / NG
        |
  Save_DB() --> DB.mdb Table_Main  (local)
             --> SQL Server Table_Main_PWR/MNL  (JA03: intended, has bug)
        |
  Barcode_Print() --> Serial_Printer ZPL
```

## 10. Shared Code Between Projects

All four station projects are **independent copies** with no shared project references. Code is duplicated across projects.

| Component | Shared Pattern | Note |
|-----------|---------------|------|
| `Common/MDBOperation.vb` | Identical across JA01–JA04 | Copy-pasted, not linked |
| `Module1.vb` structure | Load_Port_Data / Save_Port_Data / Load_BASIC_Data / Save_BASIC_Data | Nearly identical, column names differ per station |
| `HiResTimer` class | Inside Module1.vb of JA03 and JA04 | Uses `QueryPerformanceCounter` for precise timing |
| State machine pattern | `wStep`, `rStep`, `eStep` float-based ElseIf chain | Identical pattern, different step content |
| I/O module protocol | 66-char `S...E` frame, ThreadTask2 | Identical across all stations |
| Serial utility | `STR2ASC()`, `ASC2STR()` | Copy-pasted in all FrmMain.vb |
| Sound playback | `NG()` / `DingDOng()` using SoundPlayer | Identical across all stations |

---

## 11. Known Issues / Notes

| # | Issue | Location |
|---|-------|----------|
| 1 | SQL Server password hardcoded in plain text | All `Common/MDBOperation.vb`, `SERVER SEARCH/FrmMain.vb` |
| 2 | JA03 `Save_DB()` opens MDB but calls `Connection_Close()` (SQL close) instead of `Connection_Close_MDB()` | `JA03/FrmMain.vb` line 1194 |
| 3 | JA03 `Save_DB()` saves fields named `JA04_*` — field names are for JA04 data, used in JA03 | `JA03/FrmMain.vb` lines 1174–1188 |
| 4 | Float-based step numbers (e.g. `wStep = 2.1`) — floating-point comparison is unreliable | All FrmMain.vb |
| 5 | `ThreadTask1/3` use `Do While 1` — no exit condition, thread runs until serial port closes | `JA01/FrmMain.vb` |
| 6 | No global exception handler or crash.log mechanism | All projects |
| 7 | All station projects are independent copies — no shared DLL or code link | Solution structure |
